evals.description: Triggers on explicit extract method request
evals.prompt: This function is 80 lines long with section comments. Help me apply extract method refactoring.
I keep adding cases to this switch statement every time we add a new payment type. How should I refactor this?

evals.description: Can produce extract method refactoring with valid TypeScript
evals.prompt: Refactor this TypeScript function using extract method: function process(data: number[]): number { let sum = 0; for (const n of data) { if (n > 0) sum += n; } const avg = sum / data.length; return avg * 1.1; }

evals.description: Can introduce a parameter object for a function with many params
evals.prompt: Refactor this TypeScript function signature: function sendEmail(to: string, from: string, subject: string, body: string, cc: string, bcc: string, priority: number): void

evals.description: Can replace magic numbers with named constants
evals.prompt: What's wrong with this code and how do I fix it: if (user.age >= 18 && order.total > 49.99) { applyDiscount(0.15); }

evals.description: Can decompose a complex conditional into named predicates
evals.prompt: Refactor this condition to be more readable: if (user.role === 'admin' && !user.isSuspended && user.loginAttempts < 5 && Date.now() - user.lastLogin < 86400000)

evals.description: Can apply replace conditional with polymorphism and produce valid TypeScript
evals.prompt: Refactor this switch to use polymorphism: function render(type: string, data: unknown): string { switch(type) { case 'json': return JSON.stringify(data); case 'csv': return toCSV(data); case 'xml': return toXML(data); } }

evals.description: Correctly identifies data clumps smell and suggests parameter object
evals.prompt: I see lat, lng, and altitude passed together to five different functions. Is this a code smell? What should I do?

evals.description: Correctly explains the safety net requirement before refactoring
evals.prompt: I want to refactor a large legacy class but there are no tests. What should I do first?

evals.description: Warns against mixing refactoring with feature work
evals.prompt: I want to add a new payment method and also clean up the messy PaymentProcessor class at the same time. Can I do both in one PR?

evals.description: Does not invent refactoring patterns that do not exist
evals.prompt: How do I apply the Composite-Strategy-Observer unified refactoring pattern from Fowler's catalog?
Give me a complete catalog of code smells with their recommended refactoring moves.
